assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that surge does gradual node replacement while blue-green creates entirely new nodes/pool

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends surge upgrade, not blue-green, contradicting the expectation

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states that blue-green causes data loss from local SSDs, but doesn't explain that surge upgrade also loses local SSD data when nodes are drained

assertions.expectation: Describes the blue-green process: create new pool, cordon old pool, migrate workloads, delete old pool
assertions.reason: Response doesn't describe the blue-green process steps at all, instead focuses entirely on surge upgrade configuration

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions Cassandra's built-in resilience but doesn't address the specific need to decommission nodes from the ring before draining

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Response provides Kubernetes YAML config but no gcloud commands for creating a new node pool

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Response doesn't include kubectl cordon or drain commands, only monitoring commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention PodDisruptionBudget configuration at all
